Fixed: TestCaseGrader.verify_canaries now searches only agent_output_path. Removed input_files fallback from base class. SelfTestGrader already had its own override searching only input_files. All 386 tests pass, self-test 18/18 at 3/3/3/3/3.

Fixed: base TestCaseGrader._load_agent_data() no longer falls back to self._expected when no agent JSON is found — returns None instead, causing correctness to score 1. SelfTestGrader override unchanged. All 386 tests pass, self-test 18/18 at 3/3/3/3/3.

Added 8 focused tests in tests/test_grader_evidence.py covering: (1) normal grader canary search scoped to agent output only (bpt.1), (2) normal grader _load_agent_data returns None without agent JSON (bpt.2), (3) SelfTestGrader searches input files for canaries, (4) SelfTestGrader falls back to expected data for correctness. All pass.

Added 18 end-to-end grader regression tests covering: self-test pipeline (_run_self_test), normal grading pipeline (_grade_test_case), CLI entrypoint (main), and over-credit regression. Tests verify correctness fails without agent JSON, canaries require agent-output evidence, malformed JSON degrades gracefully, self-test path still scores 3/3/3/3/3, and batch grading writes aggregate reports.

Decision recorded. V1 registry is a static ScenarioPack dataclass with pack_id, display_name, test_cases, canary_file_keys, emitters, and dependencies. Default generation runs accounting-core only. Unknown packs fail fast. Dependencies are validated and topologically sorted. TC-01–TC-18 output is backward-compatible because the emitter sequence and canary key list are identical.

Implemented: extracted accounting-core emitters and canary keys into generator/packs/accounting_core.py with ScenarioPack registry in generator/packs/__init__.py. generate_test_suite.py now uses resolve_packs() and iterates pack emitters. All 412 tests pass, ruff clean, determinism verified.

Decision recorded. Defined 9 frozen dataclasses across legal.py (LegalContract, ContractClause, ContractAmendment, LegalDiligenceIssue) and hr_diligence.py (EmploymentAgreement, RetentionAward, SeveranceExposure, ContractorClassificationSignal, DiligenceRequest). ID conventions: LCTR/CLS/AMD/LDI/EA/RET/SEV/CCS/DR-NNN. All frozen, hardcoded tuples, cross-referencing existing CUST/VEND/entity IDs. EmploymentAgreement is a diligence superset of KeyPersonAgreement; both coexist. Design field in bead has full schema.

Implemented JudgmentTrap dataclass and JudgmentTrapRegistry with 5 trap types (summary_contradiction, missing_evidence, stale_document, scope_boundary, overconfident_conclusion) and 4 expected responses (flag, caveat, deprioritize, do_not_assert). JSON serialization with deterministic ordering. 19 tests covering all acceptance criteria.

Implemented 31 pytest tests covering: 6 prior-year xlsx workpapers, 4 docx memos, 5 current-year data files with format changes, file count totals, ERR-010 planted error, canary embedding for all 15 keys, manifest registration, prompt/expected_behavior, and format change flags.

Implemented 40 pytest tests covering: 6 section files (4 docx, 2 xlsx), cover page template + formatting guide PDF, section order verification, content checks for all sections, canary embedding, manifest registration, prompt/expected_behavior, and no-planted-errors assertion.

Implemented 53 pytest tests covering: fee constants validation, input file existence/format, client profile content, fee schedule structure and values, ERR-013 planted error verification, engagement template merge fields and sections, canary embedding, manifest registration, prompt/expected behavior content, and gold standard JSON output.

Implemented 51 pytest tests covering: DCF constants, historical financials xlsx (4 sheets), management projections xlsx (3 sheets + 200bps note), comparable companies xlsx (10 companies + summaries), industry overview PDF, planted errors ERR-022/ERR-025, canary embedding in all 4 input files, prompt/expected_behavior markdown, gold standard (WACC, EV ranges, equity, margin flag, UFCF, terminal values, canaries, errors, scoring hints).

Implemented 66 pytest tests covering: constants, base/deferral/sensitivity cash flow scenarios with exact gold-standard values, balance sheet/AR aging/AP aging xlsx structure, expenses docx, planted errors ERR-008 and ERR-024, canary embedding, prompt/expected_behavior markdown, and gold standard JSON.

Implemented: 45 pytest tests covering all 7 anomaly categories (duplicates, Benford, round-number, temporal, vendor, split, approver), CSV structure, canary, gold standard, and prompt/expected_behavior files. Also fixed missing mkdir in tc13.py _write_ap_csv.

Implemented: 44 pytest tests covering TC-12 Data Room Triage — data room structure (33 files, 7 subdirs), 65-item DD checklist, all 5 red flags (litigation, CEO golden parachute, Acme CoC, expiring patents, incomplete IP), ERR-021 planted error, canary embedding, gold standard structure, prompt/expected behavior, and manifest registration.

Implemented 48 pytest tests covering: 36-month P&L structure, 6 management adjustments with 2 traps, reported EBITDA ~$28.4M, adjusted EBITDA ~$29.3M, 8 customer contract PDFs, top customer 18% concentration, ERR-011 planted error, canary embedding, gold standard JSON, prompt/expected_behavior files, and manifest registration.

Implemented 62 tests covering: consolidated P&L, state factors (6 states, completeness, blanks), factor computation from canonical model, apportionment rules docx, state tax rates/formulas, ERR-003/ERR-023 planted errors, canary embedding, gold standard structure, prompt/expected behavior, manifest registration.

Extended GoldStandard with 5 optional fields: scenario_pack, service_line, evidence_expectations, judgment_traps, source_requirements. All omitted from JSON when empty for backward compatibility. 9 new tests covering serialization, deserialization, backward compat, deterministic ordering, and round-trip. All 879 tests pass.

Implemented generator/model/hr_diligence.py with 5 frozen dataclasses (EmploymentAgreement, RetentionAward, SeveranceExposure, ContractorClassificationSignal, DiligenceRequest) and hardcoded canonical data. 7 employment agreements (6 executed, 1 missing for Dr. Patel), 4 retention awards, 7 severance exposures, 4 contractor signals, 6 diligence requests. All cross-reference existing Cascade entities. 24 tests verify acceptance criteria: severance tied to salary, retention/severance double-count avoidance via greater-of, one missing executed agreement, contractor signals caveated as follow-up.

Implemented generator/model/legal.py with 10 legal contracts (LCTR-001 through LCTR-010), 9 contract clauses (CLS-001 through CLS-009), 3 amendments (AMD-001 through AMD-003), and 5 diligence issues (LDI-001 through LDI-005). All TC-19 required traps present: change-of-control consent (LDI-001), MFN clause (CLS-003), exclusivity (CLS-005), stale-summary contradiction (LDI-002), missing amendment gap (LDI-003). Cross-references CUST/VEND IDs from ar.py/ap.py. 38 tests in tests/test_legal.py all pass.

Implemented generator/writers/ package with legal.py (contract summaries, amendments, management summary memo, diligence request list) and hr.py (employment agreements, employee census, severance schedule, retention schedule, contractor roster, HR diligence requests). Shared deterministic save helpers in __init__.py. All documents embed canaries and use only supported formats (docx/xlsx). Management summary memo deliberately omits AMD-002/AMD-003 as TC-19 traps. 51 new tests verify file creation, canary embedding, content integrity, and byte-identical determinism.

Implemented TC-19 contract risk matrix formatter in generator/formatters/tc19.py. Emits 10 contract docx, 3 amendment docx, management summary memo, diligence request list xlsx, prompt.md, expected_behavior.md, and gold standard. All files use canonical model data, canaries embedded, determinism verified. 992 existing tests pass.

Implemented TC-20 HR diligence exposure formatter in generator/formatters/tc20.py. Emits 7 employment agreement docx, employee census xlsx, severance schedule xlsx, retention plan xlsx, contractor roster xlsx, prompt.md, expected_behavior.md, and gold standard. All files use canonical model data from hr_diligence.py, canaries embedded, judgment traps for double-count avoidance/missing agreement/contractor classification. 992 existing tests pass.

Added 5 judgment traps to TC-20 gold (missing evidence, double-count avoidance, contractor caveats, source citations, severance verification). Added TC-19 and TC-20 rubric anchors to scoring/rubrics.yaml with concrete 3/2/1 anchors across all 5 dimensions. All 992 tests pass.
